hypothesis: baseline
P10: parallelize+hoist segmented_reduce
P11: slice-borrow reduce_dense + Dense fast path in segmented_reduce
P12: parallelize CpuBackend::bind inputs via par_iter_mut
P13: combine P12 parallel bind + new interpolate_inplace_many trait parallelizing InstanceBind 16K calls
P14: lower rayon PAR_THRESHOLD (1024->256) and with_min_len (2048->256) in reduce_dense_fixed to capture parallelism for reduce_dense_4x4 (70.9% self-time at avg half=246)
outcome: flat (rerun confirmed, inconclusive band on slightly slower side)
notes: per-shape instrumentation confirmed 4x4 is the dominant reduce shape (81933/83430 calls); lowering task granularity at D=4 log_t=12 eats speedup due to rayon fan-out overhead vs small work/task

hypothesis: P17: fuse segmented_reduce — replace per-outer reduce_dense dispatches with inlined const-generic loop across active outer positions, eliminating ~84k Vec<&Buf> allocations, ~84k outer-slice clones, and ~84k reduce_dense dispatch overhead
notes: structurally sound (Explore agent confirmed core uses Gruen split-eq to avoid per-outer iteration entirely); but fused CPU version lost the inner rayon parallelism that per-outer reduce_dense_fixed had via PAR_THRESHOLD gate. Early rounds have LARGE inner_size + FEW active outer positions, so parallelizing only across outer positions (not inner pairs) caused a throughput regression that exceeded the per-call overhead savings at late rounds. A correct structural fix needs to dispatch on (active_count, inner_size) shape — parallelize inner when inner_size >= PAR_THRESHOLD, parallelize outer when outer dominates, or both.

hypothesis: P18: shape-aware fused segmented_reduce — P17 fusion restructured with nested rayon preserving inner par_iter via `if inner_half >= PAR_THRESHOLD` gate; outer par_iter when active>=2
notes: Fresh iter 7 Perfetto trace showed segmented_reduce wrapper = 9.4% thread time (2014ms), 100% wrapper overhead (Vec<&Buf>, outer-slice clones, self.reduce dispatch × 84k calls). Eliminated in P18 via const-generic inline loop with stack scratch. Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green, clippy clean. Perf: fusion eliminated wrapper thread time but wall-time contribution was absorbed by existing outer par_iter (wrapper work was already parallel across threads). Per-call overhead ~24µs/call × 84k calls amortized by 8-way parallelism = ≈ wrapper / effective_parallelism, within run-to-run variance. Structural remaining opportunity: port core's GruenSplitEqPolynomial so segmented sumchecks emit one cubic per round rather than N per-outer reduces.

hypothesis: P19: port folded-slot [u128;9] representation to WideAccumulator — replace carry-propagating Limbs<9> fmadd/acc_add/merge with independent u128 += u128 ops deferring carry propagation to a single normalize() at reduce() time
notes: Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green, 166/166 jolt-field green, clippy clean. Perf: two runs +1.65% / -0.45%, avg +0.60% vs 4054.58ms baseline — flat within noise. Theory predicted 10-20% given ~80M acc_add/fmadd calls per proof and 2-5× arithmetic savings per op from eliminating carry-propagation dependency chains. Observed signal was below threshold. Diagnosis: the accumulator arithmetic is NOT the reduce_dense bottleneck — F::mul inside toom_cook::eval_prod_4_assign (4 Montgomery-reducing multiplications per inner iter) dominates reduce_dense self-time, and the per-op acc_add cost (~4 cycles) is a small fraction of total per-iter work (~100 cycles). Carry propagation in the Limbs<9> hot path is also well-optimized by rustc/LLVM (u64+carry chains lower to adc/addcarry sequences with good ILP on modern CPUs). Next structural target: eliminate F::mul / Montgomery reductions inside eval_prod_4_assign via an unreduced-product kernel path (P16 in queue) — that attacks the actual dominant cost in reduce_dense, not the accumulator wrapper.

hypothesis: P16: defer Montgomery reduction through eval_prod_D_assign — added accumulator-writing Toom-Cook variants accumulate_linear_prod + accumulate_prod_{2..=8,16,32} that write final pointwise products via accs[k].fmadd(a_k,b_k) instead of outputs[k]=a_k*b_k; plumbed AccEvalFn<F> + acc_eval_fn onto CpuKernel; reduce_dense_fixed skips the evaluate→acc_add roundtrip when an acc-fn is compiled; wired product-sum (P=1, D∈{2..=8,16,32}) in compile().
outcome: flat (±5% inconclusive band after rerun; consistent improvement but below 5% accept threshold)
notes: Correctness: 70/70 jolt-cpu tests green, 41/41 jolt-equivalence green, clippy clean across jolt-cpu + jolt-core (host, host+zk) + jolt-compiler + jolt-field + jolt-zkvm. Perf: two runs -4.55% / -2.34% vs 4054.58ms baseline; avg -3.45%. Both runs in ±5% band; per protocol flat → revert. Hypothesis predicted ~20-25% assuming 30-50 cycles/Mont-reduce × 4 outputs × 246 avg half × ~84k calls; observed signal 2-5%. Diagnosis: arkworks BN254 Fr Montgomery reduction runs ~15-25 cycles on modern x86 (mulx/adx-backed from_montgomery_reduce); 4 reductions/iter × ~80M iters ≈ 1.6 Gcycles = ~0.5s single-core, amortized by 8-way rayon to ~60-80ms wall — the visible band. The fmadd 4×4 schoolbook scatter in Limbs<9> also has real cost that partly consumes the Mont-reduce savings. Per-iter arithmetic is genuinely saturated here. Next structural target: attack O(N_calls) dispatch overhead — port core's GruenSplitEqPolynomial so segmented sumchecks emit ONE cubic per round instead of N per-outer reduces (~84k → ~20 calls). That collapses call count by 4000×, not per-call cost.

hypothesis: P20: fused segmented_reduce — replace per-outer reduce_dense loop with single parallel fold over flat (active×half) iteration space; eliminate Vec<&Buf> clones; fold per-outer eq weighting into fmadd
notes: Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green, clippy clean jolt-cpu+zkvm+compiler+field+compute. Perf: two runs +0.39% / +0.54% vs 4054.58ms baseline — flat, nearly zero delta. Diagnosis: fusion eliminated (a) ~5GB of Vec-clone memcpy and (b) ~83k rayon fork/join collapsed to 1 fork. Neither was actually load-bearing. Vec<&Buf> is small-metadata (16 ptrs × 83k = 1.3MB total, not 5GB — confused slice clone with data copy). Outer rayon par_iter in old segmented_reduce was already collapsing most fork/join cost. Per-outer eq multiplication was ~83k × 4 F::muls, trivial vs ~80M inner fmadd ops. CONCLUSION: segmented_reduce structure is not the problem. The problem is per-iter arithmetic intensity. Gruen split-eq advantage in core isn't avoiding iteration — it's that core uses ONE cubic eval per round with E_out_vec × E_in_vec factoring, emitting ONE cubic polynomial instead of emitting per-outer per-inner Toom-Cook points (4×4=16 field mul per inner in D=4 case). Real win requires porting the cubic-emission formula (gruen_poly_deg_3) into segmented sumcheck prover, not micro-opt on reduce. That's 30+ LOC of compiler work — violates ≤30 LOC handler rule but IS the structural fix. Mark as graduated hypothesis family: 'segmented_reduce shape opts exhausted; next attack must be at compiler/prove_sumcheck level'.

hypothesis: P21a: instrumentation-only iter — add per-stage tracing span in execute() + tag InstanceSegmentedReduce/InstanceReduce/InstanceBind/BatchRoundBegin spans with (batch, instance, kernel, round) to attribute time
notes: Diagnostic capture: stage 1 outer_remaining sumcheck (batch=0, instance=0, kernel=3) = 53% of total prove wall (1993 ms / 3774 ms) and ~15591 ms thread-time at 7.5× parallelism — a single segmented sumcheck dominates. Round costs decay classically: 989→493→246→125→63→33→19→12→7→4 ms per round. Stage 5 InstanceReduce = 460 ms (12% parallelism, Reg read-write + Ram val). Stage 7 Dory::open = 416 ms (opening proof, not sumcheck). Concentration of target: outer_remaining is the right focus for the Gruen split-eq port — any other attack ≥ 4× lower leverage. Trace: benchmark-runs/perfetto_traces/muldiv_log_t12_iter11_staged.json.

hypothesis: P24: lower with_min_len(4096)→1024 in reduce_dense_dynamic — unlocks rayon parallelism for booleanity-shape kernels (NI=10, NE=4) with half~8-32K that were getting only 2 tasks under 4096
notes: Targeted via iter 11 span attribution: stage 5 = 515ms, dominated by kernel 21 (Booleanity, batch=4 instance=1) — 14 InstanceReduce calls × 30ms avg, max 142ms first round, total 424ms. Booleanity shape (NI=1+total_d≈10, NE=4) falls to reduce_dense_dynamic path which used with_min_len(4096). For half=8192 (typical first rounds) that's 2 tasks; lowering to 1024 gives 8 tasks → unlocks 8-way parallelism on 8-core M4. Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green, clippy clean across the 8 modular crates. Perf: 3672.07ms / 3687.88ms vs 4054.58ms baseline = -9.43% / -9.05%, avg -9.24% — clears 5% accept threshold on both runs. Ratio 12.42× → 11.03× (core 333.6ms). Stall counter: 9→0. Green streak: 0→1. Same mechanism may apply to other dynamic-path kernels (hamming=(2,4), ram_ra_virtual=(4,5), inst_ra_virtual=variable).

hypothesis: P25: extend iter 12 P24 with_min_len lowering pattern — reduce_dense_fixed(2048→1024) + reduce_sparse(4096→1024). Hypothesis: dense-fixed late rounds (half∈[1024,2048]) and medium-sparse workloads similarly underparallelized
outcome: flat (±5% inconclusive band on both runs)
notes: Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green, transcript_divergence + zkvm_proof_accepted green, clippy clean jolt-cpu + jolt-core (host, host+zk). Perf: two runs -0.12% / +0.33% vs 3672.07ms baseline, avg +0.10% — flat. Diagnosis: (a) reduce_dense_fixed is dominated by kernel 3 (NI=4,NE=4) where half starts at ~2M and halves per round; late-round halves are <128 (below PAR_THRESHOLD=1024 anyway, falls to sequential path). The band 1024-2048 is a narrow transition zone visited for only 1 round per sumcheck. (b) reduce_sparse is called on sparse polynomials (RA one-hot) where either pairs.len() is very small (skips parallel branch via <PAR_THRESHOLD check at L1119) or very large (>>4096, saturates either min_len). Few workloads hit the 1024-4096 band. Lesson: the iter 12 win was shape-specific to dynamic-path (NI≈10,NE=4) where per-task work is heavy enough that 1024-pair tasks justify thread cost; smaller-NI fixed workloads already had enough parallelism or are below threshold.

Dispatches from InstanceReduce handler when kernel shape is Gruen. Dual-path for safety against old Toom-Cook emission.

hypothesis: End-to-end Gruen port for kernel 3 (RAM RW phase 1): add KernelSpec.gruen_hint + LinComboQ metadata describing q(x)=a(x)·((1+γ)b(x)+γc(x)); implement CpuBackend::gruen_segmented_reduce assembling cubic evals via gruen_cubic_evals(1, w_current, q_const, q_quad, prev_claim) instead of the 4-point Toom-Cook grid. Runtime dispatches to new method when kdef.spec.gruen_hint.is_some().
notes: Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green incl. transcript_divergence + zkvm_proof_accepted; clippy clean across 8 modular crates. Measurement: 3 runs 1866.86/2169.80/1991.98 ms (avg -45.3%, best -49.2%, median -45.8%) vs 3672.07 ms ratchet. Ratchet updated to 1866.86 ms. Ratio 11.03× → 5.90× (best) or 6.24× (median). Three key wiring fixes landed in one iter: (a) inner_only flag honored per-input inside Gruen loop — inc buffer is inner_only while ra/val are outer-indexed, so accessing inc[a*inner_size..] would overrun its 2^log_t length; (b) eq_table construction maps bit 0 to the LAST point entry processed (per CpuBackend::eq_table doc), so for LowToHigh binding of bit k the current-round challenge is at eq_challenges[len-1-k], not [k]; (c) prev_claim read from state.batch_instance_claims[batch][instance] (populated in Op::BatchRoundBegin from prior round's evals interpolated at bind challenge). Design: kernel 3 has formula Σ (1,γ,γ) × eq·ra·{val,val,inc} = eq·ra·((1+γ)val + γ·inc) with Input(0)=eq, Input(1)=ra, Input(2)=val, Input(3)=inc. The linear-combo-of-bilinear factorization q = a·(b + γ(b+c)) exactly matches the Gruen reduce_dense_gruen_deg2 primitive with pre-fused b-coefficient computation. E[i] = eq_cycle[2i]+eq_cycle[2i+1] in LowToHigh since (1-w)+w=1 cancels the current round's variable factor; current_scalar=F::one() since eq_cycle already carries all prior-round scalar prefixes from interpolate_inplace bindings. Why it beat the hypothesis bar: kernel 3 InstanceSegmentedReduce was 57% of prove wall per iter 17 trace; Gruen collapses 4-point grid + per-pair Toom-Cook extrapolation into single cubic per round, saving both arithmetic-per-pair (5 mults vs ~12) and dispatch overhead (1 gruen_cubic_evals per round vs 4x-NE Toom-Cook). Ratchet 3672 → 1867 ms corresponds to kernel 3 dropping from ~1993 ms (iter 11 attribution) to near-noise; 2005 ms saved ≈ 2× of the attributed kernel 3 cost, consistent with the per-pair arithmetic speedup compounding with reduced wrapper overhead.

hypothesis: P27: parallelize Op::Commit outer loop via rayon. Post-Gruen trace (muldiv_log_t12_iter20_post_gruen) showed Commit at 22.13% wall (508 ms) with 42 serial DoryScheme::commit calls at 12 ms avg, 720 ms CPU / 508 ms wall = 1.4x effective parallelism. Each commit already internally parallelized (tier-1 G1::msm chunks + tier-2 Pedersen via rayon) but outer loop ran serially. Fan-out across 8 cores x 8 commits-in-flight fills work-steal queue depth, collapsing serial wall into cores-wide parallel wall.
notes: Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green incl. transcript_divergence + zkvm_proof_accepted; clippy clean across 8 modular crates. Change: ~18 LOC in crates/jolt-zkvm/src/runtime/handlers.rs Op::Commit handler + 1 line import rayon::prelude. Pre-change run1 1875.99 ms (+0.49% vs 1866.86 ratchet, flat baseline confirm). Post-change run2 1444.14 ms (-22.62%), run3 1461.77 ms (-21.68%). Both runs clear +5% accept threshold. Ratchet updated to 1444.14 ms. Ratio 5.90x -> 4.38x (best). BufferProvider's &mut receiver prevents sharing provider across threads, so materialization stays sequential (cheap, ~0.1 ms per poly at log_t=12); the expensive PCS::commit runs in parallel via into_par_iter.map. Transcript append is serial in original poly order for Fiat-Shamir determinism. Memory cost: 42 x ~128KB = 5.4 MB transient Cow::Owned copies, negligible. Why picked over kernel-21 Gruen port: Explore agent confirmed kernel 21 (IncClaimReduction) is 4 independent eq*product terms with no single eq factor, structurally incompatible with plain Gruen cubic assembly. Next iter 21: re-profile post-parallel-commit to identify new top-3 (stage 1 Materialize at 13.5%, stage 5 kernel 21 claim reduction at 13.7%, stage 7 Dory open at 12.3% are remaining candidates).

hypothesis: P28: parallelize lt_evals + EqPlusOnePolynomial::evals in jolt-poly. Post-Gruen+parallel-commit trace (muldiv_log_t12_iter21_post_parallel_commit) showed Materialize family at 408 ms / 26.2% wall — five giant eq/lt/eq_plus_one table materializes in stages 1+3 totalling ~380 ms. eq_table already has a parallel path via eq.rs PAR_THRESHOLD gate; lt_table → LtPolynomial::evaluations → lt_evals was sequential; eq_plus_one_table → EqPlusOnePolynomial::evals was sequential. Doubling pattern in lt_evals (disjoint left/right pairs) and fused per-chunk pattern in eq_plus_one.evals (within each step-sized chunk, write epo[base+half_step] from eq[base], then update eq[base] and eq[base+half_step]) are clean rayon targets. Gate on num_chunks >= PAR_THRESHOLD=1024 (i.e., parallelize only the late rounds where most work concentrates).
revert: inline edit restored original sequential bodies
notes: Correctness: 160/160 jolt-poly tests green, 41/41 jolt-equivalence green incl. transcript_divergence + zkvm_proof_accepted; clippy clean across 8 modular crates + jolt-poly + jolt-core (host, host+zk). Perf: two runs -3.19% / -0.20% vs 1444.14 ms ratchet — consistent directional signal but magnitude insufficient to clear 5% accept threshold. Diagnosis: signal is real (both runs below baseline, avg -1.7%) but either (a) actual table sizes are smaller than the 2^20+ the trace's cumulative wall suggested (at log_t=12, most tables are ≤2^12, so only 2 rounds of 12 cross the PAR_THRESHOLD gate); (b) rayon fork/join overhead on par_chunks_mut(step) at small step values (2, 4 in late rounds) eats a large fraction of per-item work; (c) the 380 ms trace attribution likely includes alloc_zeroed overhead + memset for 2^20-ish tables beyond just the arithmetic loops. Next iter 22: either raw-pointer retry (skip rayon slice splitter overhead) or pivot to Dory open (258 ms / 16.5%) / kernel 21 IncClaimReduction (205 ms / 13.2%).

Perf: 5 runs 1866/1548/1455/1414/1418 ms; cold-run discarded; median of runs 2-5 = 1436 ms = -0.55% vs ratchet 1444.14. Inside flat band, reverted. Post-change op-class: Materialize 284 ms / 1.10 threads / 13.8% sat (was 297/1.02/12.7%). 24 batches formed, 3 large batches of sizes 33/41/81 covered 155/197 Materialize calls (79%). Batching structurally correct but effective parallelism only 1.02 -> 1.10 threads. Hypotheses for flat: (a) nested rayon pessimization: materialize_binding internals (eq_table, lt_table) use rayon with PAR_THRESHOLD=1024 and contend on same work-stealing queue; (b) allocator contention on concurrent Vec<F>::with_capacity; (c) individual work avg 1.5 ms below rayon granularity threshold. Lessons: next outer-dispatch parallelism attacks should prefer ops with single large body (like P33 ReduceOpenings single RLC over 40 claims) over batching many small ops.

hypothesis: P33 parallelize inner loop of rlc_combine in jolt-openings. Iter 23 op-class showed ReduceOpenings 93ms / 1.00 threads / 12.5% sat single call; inner Horner per-position update is data-parallel above a PAR_THRESHOLD=1024 gate. Added rayon dep to jolt-openings, branched rlc_combine on len >= 1024 to par_iter_mut().zip(par_iter()).for_each. Expected 5-6% wall.
notes: Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green (transcript_divergence + zkvm_proof_accepted + full suite); clippy clean across 8 modular crates + jolt-openings. Perf: 5 runs cold-first 1482/1447/1397/1435/1433 ms; median of warm runs 2-5 = 1434.24 ms = -0.69% vs ratchet 1444.14, inside flat band. Directional signal negative in 3 of 4 warm runs (-3.3%, -0.6%, -0.8%, +0.2%) but magnitude insufficient. Diagnosis: ~11% of ReduceOpenings 93ms was in the parallelized inner Horner loop (recovered 10 ms); the remaining 82 ms is in per-group provider.materialize(pi) calls (which in fused_rlc_reduce call the same eq/lt/eq_plus_one table builds that iter 23 showed at 1.02 threads) and PCS::combine_hints (MSM-like fold not inside rlc_combine). Also at log_t=12 many polynomials are below PAR_THRESHOLD=1024 so the parallel branch does not even fire for them. Lessons: next attack should parallelize the OUTER group loop in fused_rlc_reduce after drawing all rhos serially (P35 added) so per-group materialize + rlc_combine + combine_hints run in parallel across groups. Reverted code per strict protocol.

hypothesis: P35 parallel-over-groups in fused_rlc_reduce. Iter 25 post-mortem: ~82 of 93 ms ReduceOpenings wall is in per-group work (materialize + rlc_combine + combine_hints), not the inner Horner. Restructured: serial first pass drawing all rhos, then groups.into_par_iter().zip(rhos).map(...) for per-group combine. Added P: BufferProvider<F> + Sync bound through dispatch_op/execute/prove. Expected 5-8 percent wall.
outcome: flat (slight regression inside +/-5% band, reverted per protocol)
notes: Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green; clippy clean on 8 modular crates + test files. Updated 4 call-sites to add _ to turbofish for the new generic P parameter. Perf: 5 runs cold-first 1459/1456/1414/1497/1466 ms; median of warm 2-5 = 1461.26 ms = +1.19% vs ratchet. Slight regression inside flat band. Diagnosis: likely single dominant group at log_t=12 since most modular opening claims evaluate at the same stage-challenge point and collapse into one PointGroup; into_par_iter over length-1 vec adds rayon setup overhead while one worker does the work. Third consecutive failed parallelism attack (P32/P33/P35) on Materialize and ReduceOpenings at log_t=12 despite iter 23 op-class showing 1.0 threads on 500+ ms of work. Pattern suggests log_t=12 workloads are overhead-dominated for outer-parallelism attacks. Lessons: next iter 27 is instrumentation-only (log group count + per-group wall in fused_rlc_reduce) to confirm/deny the single-group hypothesis. Iter 28 re-profile at log_t=14 to see if parallelism opportunities scale with workload size. Reverted per strict protocol.

hypothesis: P36 INSTRUMENTATION (fused_rlc_reduce group structure + per-group breakdown). No behavioral change; pure telemetry. ~25 LOC in crates/jolt-zkvm/src/runtime/helpers.rs.
notes: Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green (transcript_divergence + zkvm_proof_accepted + full suite); clippy clean on 8 modular crates. Stall counter NOT incremented per iter 11/17/22/23 precedent. Run: RUST_LOG=perf_rlc=info bench muldiv --iters 1 --warmup 0 --log-t 12 --stack modular --trace-chrome iter27_perf_rlc. Findings at log_t=12: 'rlc_group group=0 poly_count=42 total_elems=688128 mat_us=2 rlc_us=9719 hint_us=83275' + 'rlc_summary groups=1 total_claims=42'. CONFIRMS P35 single-group hypothesis (1 group of 42 claims). PER-GROUP COST: materialize 2us (padded cache hit), rlc_combine 9.7ms (10%), combine_hints 83.3ms (89% of ReduceOpenings wall). Explains iter 24 flat (padded cache already amortizes Materialize for these polys), iter 25 flat (inner rlc_combine is only 10% of wall), iter 26 flat (single group, outer par_iter adds overhead). New attack target: PCS::combine_hints (Dory opening-hint MSM fold) inside jolt-dory. 83.3ms = 5.7% of total 1444ms prove wall. If lifted from ~1.0 threads to 4-6 threads → ~60ms savings = 4-5% total wall, meets order-of-magnitude bar. P38 added to queue.

hypothesis: P38 parallelize Dory combine_hints. Iter 27 perf_rlc telemetry showed combine_hints = 83.3 ms (89% of ReduceOpenings wall) at log_t=12 with 42 hints, single call. Added rayon dep to jolt-dory; rewrote combine_hints as (0..num_rows).into_par_iter().map(|r| serial inner fold).collect(). Expected 60-70 ms wall savings = 4-5% total.
notes: Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green (transcript_divergence + zkvm_proof_accepted + full suite); clippy clean on 8 modular crates. Perf: median 1491 ms vs ratchet 1444 = +3.25%. Diagnosis (flat): num_rows at log_t=12 = 2^(12-6) = 64 rows with ~1.3 ms/row of work (42 scalar_muls + 42 adds) which is dangerously close to rayon overhead floor of 10-50 us/task times 64 tasks = 0.6-3 ms total overhead. Cross-core L2 traffic on shared 387 KB hint working set adds further overhead. Single-call usage pays full par_iter setup once with no amortization. This is the 4th consecutive parallelism attack failure at log_t=12 (P32/P33/P35/P38). Pattern strongly suggests log_t=12 workloads are overhead-dominated for rayon. Next iter 29: graduate to log_t=14 via P37 re-profile to confirm parallelism becomes productive at larger per-op work.

hypothesis: P37 re-profile at log_t=14 (DONE) — sha2-chain reveals hotspot map reshuffle: Materialize+MaterializeUnlessFresh = 47.6%, DoryScheme::commit = 27%, multi_pair_g2_setup = 24.5%, reduce_dense = 32%. combine_hints drops to ~1% — no longer attack target at higher log_t. Modular diverges from core at 1.63x per 2x workload.

notes: Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green incl. transcript_divergence + zkvm_proof_accepted; clippy clean on 8-crate canonical set. Findings: mb::EqProject = 58.8% of Materialize wall (5574 ms / 82 calls / 68 ms avg, individual calls up to 2.4s) — dominates. mb::Provided = 40.2% (3810 ms, almost entirely MaterializeUnlessFresh at 82.8 ms/call avg). Everything else <1%. CpuBackend::eq_project = 4708 ms = 94.8% of mb::EqProject wall — serial nested loop over eq_table × outer_size, no rayon. New P42 queued to parallelize eq_project, expected 18-20% total wall savings at log_t=14. Stall counter NOT incremented (iter 11/17/22/23/27/31 precedent for instrumentation iters).

hypothesis: P42 parallelize CpuBackend::eq_project via rayon. iter 33 instrumentation attributed 58.8% of log_t=14 sha2-chain Materialize family wall (5574 ms / 82 calls, worst 2.4 s) to a serial nested loop. Both branches now parallelize: branch 1 (eq_table.len() == inner_size) uses par_chunks_mut on the output so threads write disjoint [k_start..k_start+chunk] ranges; branch 2 uses par_iter_mut over the output since each slot is an independent dot-product. PAR_THRESHOLD=1024 gate preserves log_t=12 serial fallback.
outcome: improvement (green — clears +5% accept on both programs)
notes: Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green incl. transcript_divergence + zkvm_proof_accepted + full suite; clippy clean on 8-crate canonical set. Handler stays 4 lines; abstraction preserved (change is internal to CpuBackend::eq_project). Ratchet updated to 1274.20 ms. Ratio at log_t=12 = 1274.20 / 316.63 = 4.02× (vs previous 4.38×). Stall counter 7 → 0. Green streak 4 → 5. Last green iter 20 → 34. At log_t=14 sha2-chain the two runs were 19234.61 / 18527.62 ms vs iter 31 baseline 24604.96 ms — avg -23.27% / best -24.70%, consistent with the P42 hypothesis that lifting eq_project from ~1 thread to ~6-8 threads recovers 4600+ ms. Next iter 35 picks up at re-profile to identify new top Materialize hotspot (likely mb::Provided / MaterializeUnlessFresh = 3810 ms at log_t=14, now the largest remaining target per iter 33 table).

Post-P42 trace attribution showed mb::Provided = 6345 ms (+67% from iter 33's 3810 ms) = #1 remaining Materialize bucket at log_t=14. Identified jolt-r1cs/src/provider.rs:54-73 as fully serial nested loop over num_cycles × constraints × sparse_entries. Hypothesized 20-25% total wall savings via outer par_chunks_mut across num_cycles (disjoint output writes, trivially parallelizable).

notes: Correctness: 41/41 jolt-equivalence green incl. transcript_divergence + zkvm_proof_accepted + full suite; clippy clean on 8-crate canonical set. Perf at log_t=12 muldiv: runs 1/2/3 = 1308.89 / 1354.49 / 1360.56 ms = +2.72% / +6.30% / +6.78% vs 1274.20 ratchet. Two of three runs past reject threshold. At log_t=14 sha2-chain: 18779.29 ms = -0.54% vs iter 34 avg 18881.12 ms (flat). DIAGNOSIS — The hypothesis that mb::Provided's 6345 ms at log_t=14 is dominated by compute_matvec was WRONG. Parallelizing compute_matvec saved ~0 ms at log_t=14 and regressed at log_t=12, which means either (a) compute_matvec is not actually where mb::Provided spends its time (the R1cs polys might be cached after first-round or routed via padded preprocessed source), (b) the cost is in Derived (derived.compute — not touched), or (c) the freshness-check cache hits most calls so compute_matvec is invoked for only a small fraction of the attributed 6345 ms. At log_t=12 muldiv, num_cycles=4096 is above PAR_THRESHOLD but per-cycle work (~32 constraints × few sparse entries) is below rayon's amortization break-even, exactly like iter 24/25/26/28 at log_t=12. Lesson: before parallelizing a suspected hotspot, INSTRUMENT the specific method with a tracing span to verify its share of the attributed wall. Next iter 36: add per-branch instrumentation to provider.materialize, R1csSource::compute match arms, and DerivedSource::compute to attribute the 6345 ms mb::Provided wall. Stall counter 0 → 1 (first attack-fail since iter 33 series).

profile_config: sha2-chain log_t=14 num-iters=4 modular-only iters=1 warmup=0
notes: Correctness: transcript_divergence PASS, zkvm_proof_accepted PASS; clippy clean on jolt-witness + jolt-r1cs libs (pre-existing unrelated test-only lint in polynomials.rs:294-295 on --all-targets). 3 RAM derived-poly arms own 99% of pm::Derived (2036 ms / 2067 ms) and ~50% of mb::Provided — ram_ra_indicator (804 ms, 2 calls) + ram_val (622 ms, 1 call) + ram_combined_ra (610 ms, 1 call). pm::R1cs is only 33.6 ms total (iter 35 P43 would have saved at most ~30 ms even at 10× speedup — correctly reverted). pm::Witness and pm::Preprocessed are noise. 1984 ms of mb::Provided is unaccounted for inside the 4 pm arms — next instrumentation target (P46). Iter 37 attack: P45 parallelize the 3 RAM arms (K*T buffers with per-cycle independent writes), expected 4-6× → save ~1.5 s at sha2-chain log_t=14 = ~8% total wall. Stall counter unchanged at 1 per iter 33/31 precedent for instrumentation-only iters.

hypothesis: P45 parallelize DerivedSource::{ram_combined_ra, ram_val, ram_ra_indicator} via par_chunks_mut gated at PAR_THRESHOLD=1024. iter 36 attribution showed these 3 arms consume 2036 ms (99% of pm::Derived, ~50% of mb::Provided) at sha2-chain log_t=14 num-iters=4. ram_combined_ra pre-buckets cycles by address then par_chunks_mut(t) over K columns; ram_ra_indicator uses par_chunks_mut(k) on cycle-major layout; ram_val extracts fill_ram_val_chunk helper and par_chunks_mut(t) over K addresses. Expected 4-6x savings = ~1.5s at log_t=14 = ~8% total wall, likely flat muldiv log_t=12 due to serial fallback.
outcome: reverted (both programs past reject threshold; muldiv +5% boundary, sha2-chain +12.1% clear reject)
notes: Correctness: transcript_divergence PASS, zkvm_proof_accepted PASS; clippy clean on jolt-witness + jolt-r1cs libs. Perf at muldiv log_t=12: runs 1303.03/1342.95/1367.80 ms = avg 1337.93 = +5.0% vs 1274.20 ratchet (at reject boundary). Pre-P45 baseline (immediately post-iter-36 commit) was 1441.75/1390.48/1356.40 = 1396.21 avg, which is +9.6% over ratchet — either iter 36 instrumentation added overhead or hardware noise. Post-P45 vs pre-P45 = -4.2% (marginal improvement) but that doesn't clear the ratchet. At sha2-chain log_t=14 num-iters=4: runs 19599.35/20796.51 = 20197.93 avg = +12.1% vs 18011.32 iter-36 baseline. Clear reject on sha2-chain. DIAGNOSIS — all 3 arms have tiny per-chunk inner work: ram_ra_indicator writes 1 field element per cycle over T=2^14 chunks (rayon dispatch per chunk >> inner work), ram_combined_ra per-col has sparse writes (same overhead/work ratio), ram_val is memory-bandwidth-bound on K*T field writes. Additional hypothesis: concurrent rayon pool contention with P42's eq_project running in other proof stages. Candidates for iter 38+: P46 attribution-gap instrumentation (1984 ms still unaccounted inside mb::Provided), P47 reduce K*T allocation size / share RamVal + RamCombinedRa storage, P48 investigate freshness-cache extension. Lesson recurring from iter 24/25/26/28: tiny-inner-work parallelization doesn't win with rayon at these sizes. Stall counter 1 -> 2. Green streak unchanged at 5. Next iter 38: P46 close the 1984 ms attribution gap.
